recalls.Description: GM Gumili Wintergreen Essential Oil has been recalled due to safety concerns related to child poisoning. The essential oil is packaged in an amber glass bottle with a black screw-on cap and features a label displaying the "GM" logo, the product name, and an image of a wintergreen plant. The accompanying box is white with blue text, providing essential information and safety instructions on the back. A black dropper cap is included with the product.
recalls.Title: GM Gumili Wintergreen Essential Oil Recall Due to Child Poisoning Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach Linkers at 347-775-2310 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT Monday through Friday, email admin@linkersecomm.com, or visit us.shoprythm.com and click on "Recall" at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled GM Gumili Wintergreen Essential Oil-Bottle and Box Front
Recalled GM Gumili Wintergreen Essential Oil-Bottle Side Label
Recalled GM Gumili Wintergreen Essential Oil-Bottle Label with UPC Code

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com by Gumili Beauty from October 2023 through November 2025 for approximately $8.

Distributors.Name: Guangzhou Feifan Kuajing Supply Chain Co., Ltd. dba Gumili Beauty, of China

Hazards.Name: The essential oil contains methyl salicylate, which is required to be in child-resistant packaging under the Poison Prevention Packaging Act (PPPA). The current packaging does not meet this requirement, increasing the risk of poisoning if ingested by children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should immediately keep the recalled essential oil out of children's reach and contact the company for a full refund or a free replacement bottle. To process the refund or replacement, consumers will need to take a photo of the product in the trash and email it to gumilius@outlook.com.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall alert for GM Gumili Wintergreen Essential Oil due to child poisoning risk. Contact for refunds or replacements.

recalls.Description: Organic Zing Birch Oil bottles are being recalled due to a serious risk of poisoning in children. The essential oil is packaged in amber bottles with black caps and features a beige and green label displaying the Organic Zing logo, 'Birch Oil,' and 'Pure & Natural.' The packaging also indicates 'Made in India' on the back. This recall is prompted by the failure to comply with the Poison Prevention Packaging Act, as the bottles are not child-resistant.
recalls.Title: Recall of Organic Zing Birch Essential Oil Due to Child Poisoning Hazard; Non-Compliant Packaging
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers should contact Linkers at 347-775-2310 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email admin@linkersecomm.com. Visit us.shoprythm.com and click on 'Recall' at the top of the page for additional details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Organic Zing Birch Essential Oil Bottle – in packaging
Recalled Organic Zing Birch Essential Oil Bottle and packaging
Recalled Organic Zing Birch Essential Oil Bottle and packaging (Back)

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from January 2024 to November 2025 for approximately $20.

Hazards.Name: Risk of poisoning due to non-child-resistant packaging containing methyl salicylate.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should secure the bottles away from children and contact Linkers for a full refund and disposal instructions. They will need to dispose of the contents and send a photo of the empty bottle to admin@linkersecomm.com.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall of Organic Zing Birch Oil due to child poisoning risk; non-compliant packaging. Contact Linkers for refunds and disposal instructions.

recalls.Description:
Zan International Trading is recalling its Lotus Joy Organic Sweet Birch Essential Oil due to a serious risk of poisoning in children. The product is packaged in 0.34 fluid ounce amber bottles featuring a black cap and a separate glass dropper. The label displays the brand name 'LotusJoy' along with 'organic', 'sweet birch', and 'essential oil', while the packaging box is blue with similar text and additional information on the back. This product does not meet the required child-resistant packaging standards, posing a significant safety risk for young children who might accidentally ingest the contents.

Consumers are urged to keep the recalled essential oil out of children's reach and contact Zan International Trading for a full refund. As part of the return process, consumers will be required to dispose of the oil safely, taking a photo of the bottle in the trash and emailing it to the company for verification.
recalls.Title: Zan International Trading Issues Recall for Lotus Joy Sweet Birch Essential Oil Due to Child Safety Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Zan International Trading at 888-5739-288 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email zantradellc@hotmail.com.

Products.Name: Lotus Joy Organic Sweet Birch Essential Oil

Recalled LOTUSJOY Sweet Birch Essential Oil Bottle - Back

Manufacturers.Name: Guangzhou Tenghui Fragrance & Flavor Co., Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Available for purchase online at Amazon.com from August 2025 to November 2025 for approximately $13.

Distributors.Name: Zan International Trading & Services LLC, of Rockville, Maryland

Hazards.Name: Risk of poisoning due to non-child-resistant packaging containing methyl salicylate.

Remedies.Name: Secure the product away from children and request a full refund from Zan International Trading.

recalls.metaDescription: Zan International Trading recalls Lotus Joy Sweet Birch Essential Oil due to child safety risks. Contact for refunds and safe disposal instructions.

recalls.Description:
Mindbodygreen is recalling its Ultimate Multivitamin+ dietary supplement bottles due to a serious safety risk. The eight-ounce amber glass bottles contain 60 capsules and are not equipped with child-resistant packaging as mandated by the Poison Prevention Packaging Act. This poses a significant risk of poisoning for young children if the supplements are ingested. The affected products have various UPCs, lot numbers, and manufacturing dates, all of which are indicated on the bottles.

Consumers are urged to keep these bottles out of children's reach and contact Mindbodygreen for a free replacement cap that meets safety standards. The supplements can still be used as directed once secured. For further details, visit Mindbodygreen's website or reach out via email.
recalls.Title: Mindbodygreen Recalls Ultimate Multivitamin+ Due to Child Poisoning Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Mindbodygreen at multi@mindbodygreen.com or visit shop.mindbodygreen.com/pages/multi-update for more information.

Images.Caption: Recalled Mindbodygreen Ultimate Multivitamin+ Dietary Supplement Bottle
Label on Mindbodygreen Ultimate Multivitamin+ Dietary Supplement Bottles

Manufacturers.Name: Arizona Nutritional Supplements, of Chandler, Arizona

Retailers.Name: Available at Mindbodygreen.com from November 2021 through November 2025, priced between $40 and $70 based on subscription.

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death from poisoning due to non-child-resistant packaging

Remedies.Name: Free child-resistant replacement cap available upon request

recalls.metaDescription: Mindbodygreen recalls Ultimate Multivitamin+ due to child poisoning risk. Secure your bottles and request a free replacement cap.

recalls.Description: RootStim is recalling its Beard Growth Serum spray bottles due to a serious risk of child poisoning. The serums are packaged in black boxes featuring orange and white text, including an orange mustache icon. The front displays "Beard Growth Treatment" along with "5% Minoxidil - Biotin & Rice Water," while directions and warnings are printed on the sides. The bottles themselves are black, equipped with a black spray cap and a transparent top cap, and are labeled with the same information found on the box.
recalls.Title: RootStim Beard Growth Serum Spray Bottles Recalled Due to Child Poisoning Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach RootStim at 202-743-1714 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email finergraceing@gmail.com, or visit https://rootstim.com and click on "Contact Us".

Products.Name: RootStim Beard Growth Serum with 5% Minoxidil

Images.Caption: Recalled RootStim Beard Growth Serum Packaging (Front)

Manufacturers.Name: Ronghui Intelligent Manufacturing Technology (Guangzhou) Co., Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from November 2024 through September 2025 for approximately $20.

Hazards.Name: Non-child-resistant packaging poses a poisoning risk to young children due to the presence of minoxidil.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should secure the serum bottles out of children's reach and contact RootStim for a replacement. The original bottle should be discarded.

recalls.metaDescription: RootStim recalls Beard Growth Serum bottles due to child poisoning risks. Secure and contact for replacement instructions.

recalls.Description:
Ruahouine is recalling its Hair Growth Serum due to a serious risk of poisoning in children. The serum is packaged in 60ml (2.11 fl. oz.) transparent amber dropper bottles labeled with "Ruahouine", "Minoxidil Hair Growth Serum", and "5% Minoxidil With Biotin". The packaging does not meet the required child-resistant standards, posing a significant danger if ingested by young children.

Consumers are urged to secure the recalled serum bottles immediately and keep them out of children's reach. Ruahouine is providing instructions for the safe disposal of the product as well as options for refunds or replacements. It is crucial to follow the outlined steps to prevent accidents and ensure safety.
recalls.Title: Ruahouine Hair Growth Serum Recall Due to Child Poisoning Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, please contact Ruahouine at ruahouine@gmail.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Ruahouine Hair Growth Serum

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from January 2025 to July 2025 for approximately $10.

Hazards.Name: Non-child-resistant packaging leading to poisoning risk

Remedies.Name: Instructions for safe disposal and refund or replacement options

recalls.metaDescription: Important recall of Ruahouine Hair Growth Serum due to child poisoning risk. Secure and dispose of the product safely.

recalls.Description:
Feel The Beard is recalling its Beard Growth Oil due to a serious risk of poisoning in children. The product is packaged in a one-ounce dark amber bottle with a black dropper cap and is labeled as "Beard Growth Oil," "Feel The Beard," and "5% minoxidil with biotin." This recall is due to the failure to meet the mandatory child-resistant packaging standards set by the Poison Prevention Packaging Act. The lack of proper packaging increases the risk of serious injury or death if ingested by young children.

Consumers are urged to keep the recalled serum bottles out of children's reach and contact Feel The Beard for guidance on how to dispose of the product safely. Instructions will be provided for destroying the bottles and obtaining a replacement. This includes disposing of the contents in the trash and sending a photo of the bottle in the trash to the provided email address.
recalls.Title: Feel The Beard Recalls Minoxidil Beard Growth Oil Due to Child Poisoning Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: Email Feel The Beard at ziyad@feelthebeard.com.

Products.Name: Feel The Beard Minoxidil Beard Growth Oil

Images.Caption: Recalled Feel The Beard Growth Oil Bottle
Recalled Feel The Beard Growth Oil Bottle (side view)

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from April 2025 to September 2025 for approximately $10.

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death from child poisoning due to non-child-resistant packaging.

Remedies.Name: Secure the bottles out of children's reach and follow instructions for safe disposal and replacement.

recalls.metaDescription: Feel The Beard recalls Minoxidil Beard Growth Oil due to child poisoning risks. Learn how to safely dispose of the product.

recalls.Description: F&F Fine Wines has announced a recall for specific glass bottles of Kirkland Signature Valdobbiadene Prosecco DOCG. These bottles, featuring a green design with purple foil and a purple label, were sold at Costco. The UPC for this product is 196633883742, and it was available under Costco Item Number 1879870. Consumers should be aware of the potential risk associated with these bottles breaking or shattering, which could lead to laceration injuries.
recalls.Title: F&F Fine Wines Issues Recall for Kirkland Signature Valdobbiadene Prosecco DOCG Due to Laceration Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Ethica Wines at 786-810-7132, available Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. EST, or email customercare@ethicawines.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Kirkland Signature Valdobbiadene Prosecco DOCG bottle

Injuries.Name: F&F Fine Wines International, Inc. has received ten reports of the bottles shattering or breaking, resulting in one laceration injury.

Retailers.Name: Costco locations in IA, IL, IN, KY, MI, MN, MO, ND, NE, OH, SD, and WI from April 2025 to August 2025 for approximately $8.

Importers.Name: F&F Fine Wines International, Inc. dba Ethica Wines, of Miami, Florida

Hazards.Name: Laceration risk due to potential breakage of bottles

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled bottles immediately, do not attempt to open them, and dispose of them in household trash. Contact Ethica Wines for refund instructions.

recalls.metaDescription: F&F Fine Wines recalls Kirkland Signature Prosecco due to laceration hazards. Stop using and contact Ethica Wines for a refund.

recalls.Description: Creekwood Naturals is recalling its Gum Spirits of Turpentine bottles due to a serious risk of poisoning in children. The product is packaged in a clear glass bottle featuring a brown label with prominent red, black, and white text, clearly stating "Turpentine" and "Pure Gum Spirits". This recall is crucial as the packaging does not meet the required child-resistant standards, posing a significant hazard to young children.
recalls.Title: Creekwood Naturals Recalls Turpentine Bottles Due to Child Poisoning Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Creekwood Naturals at 877-754-1776 from 10 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or via email at Sales@CreekwoodNaturals.com. More information is available at https://CreekwoodNaturals.com/pages/recall or by visiting https://CreekwoodNaturals.com and selecting the "Recall" option at the top.

Images.Caption: Recalled Creekwood Naturals 100% Natural Pure Gum Spirits Turpentine bottle

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon, Etsy, eBay, and Creekwood Naturals from June 2023 through May 2025, priced between $20 and $40.

Hazards.Name: Risk of poisoning to children due to inadequate child-resistant packaging

Remedies.Name: Free replacement child-resistant closure available upon request

recalls.metaDescription: Creekwood Naturals recalls Gum Spirits of Turpentine due to child poisoning risks. Learn how to secure a free replacement closure.

recalls.Description: Diamond G Forest Products is recalling its 4-ounce clear glass bottles of 100% pure gum spirits of turpentine. These bottles are round and labeled with "Georgia Pine Gum Spirits of Turpentine" and the company name. The lack of child-resistant packaging presents a significant risk of serious injury or death from poisoning if ingested by children. This recall is in violation of the Poison Prevention Packaging Act, which mandates that such products must be packaged to prevent access by young children.
recalls.Title: Diamond G Forest Products Issues Recall for Turpentine Bottles Due to Child Poisoning Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Diamond G Forest Products at 912-647-7463 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email at diamondgforestproducts@hotmail.com, or visit www.diamondgforestproducts.com/recall-information. More details can also be found by selecting "Recall" on their website.

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death from poisoning due to non-child-resistant packaging.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should secure the turpentine bottles out of children's reach and contact Diamond G Forest Products for a free replacement child-resistant closure.

recalls.metaDescription: Diamond G Forest Products recalls turpentine bottles due to child poisoning risk. Secure the product and contact for a free replacement closure.

recalls.Description: Gerolsteiner is recalling specific lots of its 750ml sparkling water bottles due to a potential laceration hazard. These bottles, sold in individual large glass containers or cases of 15, feature a white, blue, and red label bearing the name 'Gerolsteiner.' The affected lot numbers are 11/28/2024 L and 11/27/2024 L, which can be found on the lower part of the label.
recalls.Title: Gerolsteiner 750ml Sparkling Water Bottles Recalled Due to Laceration Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can contact Gerolsteiner at 800-777-0633 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email at customerservice@consup.us, or visit https://www.gerolsteiner.de/en/recall. Additional details can also be found on their website at www.gerolsteiner.de/en/ under the 'Recalls' section.

Images.Caption: Recalled Gerolsteiner Sparkling Water (750ml)
Recalled Gerolsteiner Sparkling Water (750ml) lot number location

Manufacturers.Name: Gerolsteiner Brunnen GmbH & Co. KG, of Germany

Retailers.Name: Trader Joe's locations across Alabama, Arkansas, Colorado, Florida, Georgia, Kansas, Louisiana, New Mexico, Oklahoma, South Carolina, Tennessee, and Texas from December 2024 through January 2025, priced around $3 per bottle.

Hazards.Name: Laceration hazard due to cracking bottles

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled bottles and return them to the point of purchase for a full refund, no proof of purchase needed.

recalls.metaDescription: Gerolsteiner recalls 750ml sparkling water bottles due to laceration risks. Check lot numbers and return for a refund.

recalls.Description: Euqee has issued a recall for its Wintergreen Essential Oil sold in 118 ml amber glass bottles with black screw-on caps. The front label reads, 'Euqee, Essential Oil, Premium Grade, 100% Pure Plant Extract and Wintergreen,' while the back provides safety warnings and storage guidelines. This product poses a significant risk as it contains methyl salicylate, which is required to be in child-resistant packaging under the Poison Prevention Packaging Act.
recalls.Title: Euqee Wintergreen Essential Oil Recalled for Poisoning Risk Due to Non-Child-Resistant Packaging
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should secure the recalled product away from children and reach out to DLWL-US at gzzlhus@163.com or visit www.euqee.net/page9 for further details on obtaining a full refund.

Images.Caption: Recalled Euqee Wintergreen Essential Oil

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively on Amazon.com from December 2023 through August 2024 for approximately $8.

Distributors.Name: DingLinWangLuoKeJiGuang ZhouYouXianGongSi, dba DLWL-US, of China

Hazards.Name: Non-child-resistant packaging poses a poisoning risk to young children if ingested.

Remedies.Name: Consumers can request a pre-paid return label and free packaging to send back the recalled essential oil for a full refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Euqee recalls Wintergreen Essential Oil due to poisoning risk from non-child-resistant packaging. Secure the product and request a refund.
